title: Amadeus production API switch — workload field for test|production, smoke production endpoint
description: Item #7 confirmed the Amadeus test API has persistent 5xx flake. Production API behaves better. This round adds a workload field `amadeus_env: test | production` to the Amadeus MCP playbook, plumbs the endpoint URL choice through, and runs a small production smoke (limited scope — production API usage costs real money per request). Cap: 1 ops PR.

design_brief_for_codex.current_state: repos/ops/automation/agents/mcp/amadeus.yaml has hardcoded endpoints like `https://test.api.amadeus.com/v2/shopping/flight-offers`. The travel runtime's workload has `amadeus_env: test` as a field but it's never plumbed through — the MCP playbook ignores it.
design_brief_for_codex.target_state: mcp/amadeus playbook accepts `amadeus_env: test | production` as a workload field. The four endpoint URLs (token + flights + locations + hotels + activities) switch between `test.api.amadeus.com` and `api.amadeus.com` based on the field. Default stays `test` so existing kind smokes don't change. Production credentials (different from test credentials!) are pulled from a separate secret path when `amadeus_env: production`.
design_brief_for_codex.phase_1_audit_endpoints: Read mcp/amadeus.yaml. Identify every test.api.amadeus.com URL. Document the production equivalents (api.amadeus.com per Amadeus self-service docs).
design_brief_for_codex.phase_2_workload_field_and_substitution: Add `amadeus_env: test` (default) to workload. Replace hardcoded test URLs with `{{ 'https://api.amadeus.com' if workload.amadeus_env == 'production' else 'https://test.api.amadeus.com' }}` — same Jinja conditional pattern from the keychain endpoint rule. Or use a workload-derived base URL field to keep the substitution clean.
design_brief_for_codex.phase_3_credentials_path: Production Amadeus credentials are DIFFERENT from test credentials. The workload has fields `amadeus_key_path` and `amadeus_secret_path` for the test creds. Add `amadeus_production_key_path` and `amadeus_production_secret_path` for production. The keychain entry's `map.client_id` / `map.client_secret` Jinja conditionally picks the right secret path based on `amadeus_env`.
design_brief_for_codex.phase_4_production_secrets_gate: If `amadeus_env: production` and the production secret paths point at NOT_FOUND secrets — fall back gracefully with a clear error in the audit row. Kadyapam owns provisioning the production secrets in GCP, same as Anthropic.
design_brief_for_codex.phase_5_smoke_limited:
Smoke production with TIGHTLY LIMITED scope to control cost:
  1. `cd /mcp/amadeus; call get_token` with production endpoint — verifies auth.
  2. `travel --provider openai locations near Paris` — locations is a cheap endpoint, single call.
  3. ONE flights query: `travel --provider openai flights from SFO to JFK on 2026-07-15 for 1 adult` — single offers request.
  Do NOT spam smokes. Production usage costs per-call. Capture results.
design_brief_for_codex.phase_6_close_out: Memory + sync issue update + validation log. Document production credential setup recipe for Kadyapam if production secrets weren't provisioned yet.

1_audit_endpoints.objective: Inventory test endpoint URLs in mcp/amadeus.
spec: grep test.api.amadeus.com in repos/ops/automation/agents/mcp/amadeus.yaml; document each URL + the production equivalent.
Replace test URLs with Jinja-conditional substitution OR derive base URL field.
2_workload_field_and_substitution.verify: Runtime validates. Default test mode preserves current behavior.

3_credentials_path.objective: Production credentials wired via separate secret paths.
spec: Update keychain entry's Jinja to conditionally pick the right secret path.
3_credentials_path.verify: Keychain works for both test (default) and production (when secret exists).

4_production_secrets_check.objective: Document what Kadyapam needs to provision for production smokes.
spec: Read Amadeus self-service docs for production endpoint requirements (Amadeus requires explicit production approval).
Recipe in result file: `gcloud secrets create amadeus-production-{key,secret} --project=noetl-demo-19700101 ...`.
If production secrets aren't provisioned: AMBER on smoke, document for Kadyapam, GREEN on the code change.
If production secrets exist: get_token + 1 locations call + 1 flights call. Capture results.
If not: skip phase 5, mark code-only GREEN.
5_smoke_limited.verify: Production endpoint validated OR provisioning recipe ready.

6_close_out.objective: Memory + sync issue + validation log + ai-meta pointer bump.
spec: Sync issue update — close 'Amadeus production switch' line.
Bump repos/ops gitlink. Stage, don't push.

denied_tools: Provisioning Amadeus production credentials — Kadyapam owns secret manager
Spamming the production API to validate — single-digit calls maximum
Modifying the travel runtime's classifier (this is mcp/amadeus only)
preferred_tools: Read tool on amadeus.yaml + Amadeus docs
noetl status <id> --json | jq for smoke verification
result_file_instructions: Write bridge/outbox/20260511-230000-amadeus-production-api-switch.result.json. Required fields: {id, from, to, completed_at, overall_status, phases: {1_audit_endpoints: {urls_found}, 2_workload_field_and_substitution: {validates}, 3_credentials_path: {keychain_updated}, 4_production_secrets_check: {recipe_documented, production_secrets_exist}, 5_smoke_limited: {executed, get_token_status, locations_status, flights_status}, 6_close_out: {ops_sha, ai_meta_commit_local, sync_issue_updated}}, verdict, notes, deferred_followups}.
